title: Iran: Production of Metals, Nonmetals and Non-Oil Mineral Fuels, 1961-1965 + 1968-1970 + 1973-1980 (USGS/Bureau of Mines Minerals Yearbook, transcribed from 4 historical volumes)
source_org: US Bureau of Mines (Minerals Yearbook Volume III/IV, Area Reports: International), authored by David A. Carleton (1970 chapter) and successors
source_url: https://archive.org (see parent folder manifest for exact volume URLs)
extraction_method: MANUAL TRANSCRIPTION from `pdftotext -layout` output of the Iran chapter page ranges in each of the 4 downloaded volumes (see parent manifest for exact page ranges). These tables were NOT visually PNG-verified because the text layer was cleanly legible and well-aligned in narrow single-column commodity/value format (unlike the wide landscape World Bank dam tables from the same round, which did require visual verification) -- spot-checked several rows against the surrounding narrative prose (e.g. 'Chromite production advanced to a new high of about 150,000 metric tons' in the 1965 report's Commodity Review matches the Table 1 chromite=150,000 figure for 1965 exactly), which is treated as sufficient cross-validation for this cleanly-formatted source. One explicit unit ambiguity is flagged in data.csv (Iron ore 1968-1970 rows) where the 1970 volume's table dropped its unit marker in the OCR/text layer -- transcribed as printed with the ambiguity disclosed rather than guessed.
time_coverage: 1961-1965, 1968-1970, 1973-1980 (near-continuous; gaps only at 1966-1967 and 1971-1972, corresponding to volumes not selected for download in this round)
units: metric tons unless otherwise noted per-row (thousand metric tons flagged explicitly where the source table specifies it)
notes: data.csv is the long-format production time series (category/commodity/unit/year/value/flag/source_yearbook_edition) covering METALS (chromite, copper, iron ore, pig iron, crude steel, aluminum, lead, manganese, zinc), NONMETALS (barite, cement, gypsum, salt, sulfur) and non-oil MINERAL FUELS (coal, coke) -- crude petroleum and refined petroleum products deliberately EXCLUDED from this dataset since they are extensively covered by other datasets in this project already (this dataset's whole purpose is the NON-OIL mining gap). commodity-narrative-highlights.csv captures the qualitative Commodity Review sections: the Sar Cheshmeh copper mine's full history arc across the 4 snapshots is a standout find -- 1965 (pre-discovery exploration phase) -> 1970 ($330 million open-pit development formally approved, Iranian Selection Trust/Kerman Mining Co joint venture, 350 million tons reserves at 1.2% Cu) -> 1975 (construction underway, delayed, 145,000 tons/year full-capacity target) -> 1980 (cost ballooned to $1.4 billion / 450 million tons at 1.13% Cu, construction essentially complete but OPERATIONS SUSPENDED after the Islamic Revolution caused foreign personnel withdrawal, output redirected from export to domestic-only). Parallel arc for the Isfahan Steel Mill (Pahlavi-era name 'Aryamehr Iron and Steel Complex', Soviet-built): 1965 (planning phase, USSR gas-for-steel-mill barter deal, Shah Abbas/Zayandeh Rud Dam built partly to supply the mill's water -- direct cross-reference to this round's wb1975water-major-dams-specifications-1971 dataset) -> 1970 (under construction, trial production April 1971, 1.5M ton/year initial target) -> 1975 (operational at 750,000 tons/year actual capacity, expansion to 8M tons/year planned by 1986) -> 1980 (1.5M tons/year reported capacity but producing under half due to coal-supply problems, post-revolution). Cross-validated production figures against the narrative text wherever both were present in the same chapter (e.g. 1965 chromite, 1970 iron/steel narrative). Part of this project's Round 38 (mining/dams/industrial subsectors) hunt -- this dataset is the primary deliverable for the 'mining (non-oil)' mission component.
